mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity constraints and impending corporate margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 0.5% establishes a deeply accommodative financial environment that compresses equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining total nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based labor market deterioration that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent UNRATE increases and PAYEMS decreases fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly expands aggregate corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no positive predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 direction, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that rate hikes are successfully choking off economic activity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, with directional accuracy below 45% in out-of-sample tests.
